steps.title: Identify the relationship
steps.content:
Before the blank: actors showed suppressed brain responses during performances.
After the blank: actors' responses were normal in nonacting contexts.

The two sentences contrast performing with not-performing — opposite results in opposite contexts.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is contrast. Sentence 1: actors showed suppressed brain responses during performances. Sentence 2: actors' responses were normal in nonacting contexts. The two findings are opposites — suppressed vs. normal, performing vs. not performing. The blank needs a transition signaling a reversal.
  • Choice A — "specifically,": signals that Sentence 2 elaborates or narrows Sentence 1 — but the two sentences describe opposite results, not one being a detail of the other. ✗
  • Choice B — "likewise,": signals similarity — but the results are opposite (suppressed vs. normal). ✗
  • Choice C — "thus,": signals cause-and-effect — Sentence 2 does not result from Sentence 1; they are parallel findings in different contexts. ✗
  • Choice D — "conversely,": signals an opposite or reversed relationship — exactly matching suppressed-in-performance vs. normal-outside-performance. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
